chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2613 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يُصِيبُ ثَوْبَهُ زَعْفَرَانُ اَلْكَعْبَةِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ هُوَ طَهُورٌ فَلاَ تَتَّقِهِ أَنْ يُصِيبَكَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2613 - Sama'ah asked Imam (as) about a man whose garment is stained with the saffron of the Ka'bah while he is in the state of Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it; it is pure, so do not avoid it if it touches you."
